name: Seattle-Style Cioppino
recipeIngredient: Red Bell Pepper (1 medium, stemmed and seeded, chopped)
Green Bell Pepper (1 medium, stemmed and seeded, chopped)
Whole Tomatoes in Juice (28 oz, coarsely chopped)
Yellow Bell Pepper (1 yellow, stemmed and cut into chunks)
Live Mussels (1 lb, scrubbed and beards removed)
Red Snapper (1 1/2 lb, cut into chunks)
Cooked Crab (1/2 lb, cleaned and cracked)
Calamari Mantles (1/2 lb, cut into rings)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sweet Pepper Sauce: Heat olive oil over medium heat in a large skillet. Add peppers, onions, and garlic; sauté until soft, about 10 minutes.
2. Add basil, oregano, tomatoes, clam juice, and wine. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for 1 hour, stirring occasionally. Keep warm.
3. Cioppino: Heat olive oil over medium-high heat in a large Dutch oven or kettle. Add yellow pepper, onion, and garlic; sauté until soft, about 5 minutes.
4. Add tomato and lemon juice; cook 2 minutes.
5. Add mussels, clams, fish, and shrimp. Cover and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes.
6. Add crab and Sweet Pepper Sauce. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes. Stir in calamari.
7. Cover and cook for 2-3 minutes longer or until calamari is opaque throughout, shrimp is pink and opaque, fish is cooked through, crab is hot, and mussels and clams have opened.
8. Discard any unopened mussels or clams.
9. Serve cioppino in tureen or large soup bowls, dividing various ingredients among the bowls.
